# Code of Conduct

## Our Commitment

We want this project to be a welcoming and respectful space for everyone.
Contributors, maintainers, and community members are expected to act in ways
that support a harassment-free experience regardless of age, body size,
disability, ethnicity, gender identity and expression, level of experience,
nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, or sexual identity and
orientation.

## Expected Behavior

- Be respectful and constructive.
- Assume good intent, and ask clarifying questions before escalating.
- Give and receive feedback in a professional way.
- Focus discussion on technical outcomes and project goals.

## Unacceptable Behavior

- Harassment, discrimination, intimidation, or personal attacks.
- Insults, trolling, inflammatory comments, or deliberate disruption.
- Sharing private information without explicit permission.
- Any conduct that would be considered inappropriate in a professional setting.

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies in all project spaces, including issues, pull
requests, discussions, and other public or private communication channels tied
to this repository.

## Enforcement

Project maintainers are responsible for clarifying and enforcing this Code of
Conduct. Reports can be made by opening a private security advisory in GitHub
(Security tab) or, if unavailable, by contacting maintainers through a private
channel.

Maintainers may take any action they consider appropriate, including warning,
removal of content, or temporary/permanent bans from participation.
